# stat-api .NET SDK

Official .NET client for the [stat-api](https://stat-api.com) sports data API —
a single, typed surface over NBA, NFL, MLB, NHL, and PGA data.

Zero package dependencies (net8.0 `HttpClient` + `System.Text.Json` from the
shared framework). Version 0.1.0.

## Install

NuGet publishing is deferred. Install from source — the project lives in the
[`dotnet/` subdirectory](https://github.com/stat-api/stat-api/tree/main/dotnet):

```xml
<ProjectReference Include="path/to/dotnet/StatApi.csproj" />
```

## Authenticate

The client reads your API key from the `STAT_API_KEY` environment
variable, or you can pass it directly:

```csharp
using StatApi;

var client = new StatApiClient();                 // reads STAT_API_KEY
var keyed = new StatApiClient(apiKey: "sdb_...");  // or pass it in
```

Point at a different host with `baseUrl:` or `STAT_API_BASE_URL`
(default `https://api.stat-api.com`).

## List, get, and iterate

```csharp
// One page.
var page = await client.Nba.Players.ListAsync(new NbaPlayersListParams { Limit = 25 });
foreach (var player in page.Rows)
{
    Console.WriteLine(player.FullName);
}

// Fetch one row by id.
var one = await client.Nba.Players.GetAsync(201939);

// Auto-paginate the whole result set (walks next_from_id for you).
await foreach (var p in client.Nba.Players.IterateAsync(new NbaPlayersListParams { TeamId = 1 }))
{
    Console.WriteLine(p.FullName);
}
```

Rows are `record`s — access fields via Pascal properties (`player.TeamId`).
High-precision columns (dates, timestamps, decimals) arrive as `string` so no
precision is lost on the wire.

## Errors

Every non-2xx response throws a typed `StatApiException` subclass; the raw
server envelope is on `.Body`.

```csharp
try
{
    await client.Nba.Players.GetAsync(-1);
}
catch (NotFoundException)
{
    // ...
}
catch (QuotaExceededException ex)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"quota resets at {ex.ResetsAt} -> {ex.UpgradeUrl}");
}
catch (StatApiException ex)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"{ex.Status} {ex.Body}");
}
```

| Status | Exception |
|-------|-----------|
| 400 | `ValidationException` |
| 401 | `AuthenticationException` |
| 402 | `PlanRequiredException` |
| 404 | `NotFoundException` |
| 429 | `QuotaExceededException` |
| other | `StatApiException` |

## Quota

Every response carries your monthly record quota. It's on each `Page` and on
`QuotaExceededException`:

```csharp
var page = await client.Nba.Players.ListAsync(new NbaPlayersListParams { Limit = 1 });
if (page.Quota is not null)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"{page.Quota.Remaining} of {page.Quota.Limit} left");
}
```
